LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JAMES BRADEN & LAW OFFICES OF JAMES M. BRADEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE CORPORATE PLAINTIFFS DID NOT NEED TO OBTAIN A CERTIFICATE OF QUALIFICATION TO FILE THE INSTANT SUIT. CORPORATIONS CODE 191 EXCLUDES THE MAINTENANCE OR DEFENSE OF A LAWSUIT FROM THE DEFINITION OF INTRASTATE BUSINESS. FILING A LAWSUIT IS PART OF THE MAINTENANCE PROCESS. FURTHER, JUDICIAL NOTICE OF A "SCREEN SHOT" FROM THE SECRETARY OF STATE DOES NOT ESTABLISH THE TRUTH OF THOSE MATTERS. AS FOR THE CONDUCT OF OTHER INTRASTATE BUSINESS SOUGHT TO BE ESTABLISHED BY JUDICIAL NOTICE OF VARIOUS MATTERS ASSERTED IN PRIOR LAWSUITS, THE TRUTH OF SUCH MATTERS CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED BY JUDICIAL NOTICE. PLAINTIFF SEZERMAN SUFFICIENTLY STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LEGAL MALPRACTICE AND A FACTUAL ISSUE REMAINS REGARDING WHETHER HE WAS DAMAGED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL KECK, ESQ., 415.861.2267, FOR PLAINTIFF OZ OPTICS, LIMITED.
KAREN STROMEYER, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR DEFENDANT LAW OFFICES OF JAMES BRADEN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTs JAMES BRADEN & LAW OFFICES OF JAMES M. BRADEN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice. The Court overrules the demurrer in its entirety. The corporate plaintiffs did not need to obtain a certificate of qualification to file the instant suit. Corporations Code 191 excludes the maintenance or defense of a lawsuit from the definition of intrastate business. Filing a lawsuit is part of the maintenance process. Further, judicial notice of a "screen shot" from the Secretary of State does not establish the truth of those matters. As for the conduct of other intrastate business sought to be established by judicial notice of various matters asserted in prior lawsuits, the truth of such matters cannot be established by judicial notice. Plaintiff Sezerman sufficiently states a cause of action for legal malpractice and a factual issue remains regarding whether he was damaged. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2013 Continued To Aug-12-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTs JAMES BRADEN and LAW OFFICES OF JAMES M. BRADEN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Judge Miller is recused. Per the order of the Presiding Judge, the motion is continued to August 12, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
